design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate platforming stages while managing depleting health, collect weapons and power-ups, reach checkpoint safety, defeat boss at stage 4 of each area
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Health depletion over time creates constant pressure; one-hit-death mechanic from enemies/hazards forces precision; weapon scarcity demands strategic pickup timing
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Rescue the princess from an island of dangers as Master Higgins; gradual empowerment through discovered weapons and items transforms player from defenseless to capable
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unforgiving one-hit-death enemy contact combined with passive health drain (no standing still) creates a pressure-cooker platformer that rejects idle gameplay. Weapon scarcity and hidden egg discovery encourage experimentation. Checkpoint system softens difficulty while maintaining tension.

difficulty.lives_system: 3 lives starting; extra lives awarded at 50k, 100k, 200k points. Death on enemy contact, hazard touch, or health depletion to zero
difficulty.continues_system: Game over when all lives exhausted; stages do not continue from boss defeats
difficulty.checkpointing: 4 checkpoints per stage; respawn at last checkpoint upon death maintains progression safety but stage must be replayed from that point
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; health depletion is automatic and cannot be paused. Players can seek health items (fruit, milk) and invincibility power-ups (Honey Girl), but timing is constrained by game pacing

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon acquisition: unarmed → stone axe (found in stage) → magical fireballs (trade axe). Health upgrades via fruit/milk bottles (max 16 points). Invincibility frames via Honey Girl fairy. Score bonuses via hidden pots and bonus stages
progression.unlock_mechanism: 8 areas × 4 rounds each; each area 4 locked behind defeating that area's boss form. Progression is stage-sequential with no branching. Hidden eggs and bonus clouds are discoverable but optional
replayability_hooks: Hidden egg locations and bonus stage warps require skill/knowledge to locate
Score chase mechanic (extra lives at 50k/100k/200k points)
Double bonus pot hidden in each stage adds risk/reward element
32 total stages provide extended challenge for perfectionists

world_building_techniques: Princess Tina kidnapping establishes rescue motivation (manual instruction)
Evil Witch Doctor as repeated boss reinforces single antagonist across 8 forms
Island setting (South Pacific) and visual theming provide environmental context
Character rename/localization (Takahashi Meijin → Master Higgins) reflects regional adaptation without in-game narrative shifts

boss_design.philosophy: Single antagonist (Evil Witch Doctor) with escalating durability and speed; emphasizes pattern recognition and endurance rather than mechanic novelty. Eight forms create sense of climactic progression. Boss appears only at stage 4 of each area, making encounters special
notable_bosses: Evil Witch Doctor (Form 1-8): Head-swapping antagonist requiring 8 hits initially, scaling to 22 hits by final form. Consistent attack pattern (fireball spam) with increased speed/durability per form. Defeats trigger escape to next area rather than stage clear

danger_signals: One-hit-death from enemies signals high-stakes platforming difficulty
Automatic health depletion creates visible time pressure (health bar drain)
Boss durability scaling (8→10→12→...→22 hits) signals game difficulty ramping
Limited lives (3 starting) with finite continues emphasizes consequence
environmental_storytelling: Island stages with varied tropical/hazard themes establish Adventure Island as hostile environment
Pitfalls, rolling stones, fire roasts, and water hazards communicate environmental danger without text
Checkpoint placement (4 per stage) implicitly guides player through intended routes
Egg containers as universal collectible establish exploration/interaction convention
